title: orchis sulphurea subsp. castellana
intro: Orchis sulphurea subsp. castellana is a subspecies of orchid that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive growth habit. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, which are believed to include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with this plant are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or salves, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied or proven.
